page: Microsoft Azure D4ls_v5 is an x86_64 architecture virtual server belonging to the DLSv5 family. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz, with 2 physical cores and 2 threads per core. The system is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is premium storage capable and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates moderate single-core capabilities but limited multi-core scaling. The single-core stress-ng and Geekbench scores fall within the average range, as do single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench is weak, placing in the bottom 25%. Memory performance is a notable bottleneck, with Passmark memory scores and cached read bandwidth testing in the poor and weak categories respectively, though uncached write bandwidth remains average.

Given its low memory-to-CPU ratio and lack of local storage, this server represents a budget-conscious option for compute-bound tasks that do not require large memory footprints. It is suitable for lightweight web serving, where it achieves average static web serving throughput, and basic single-threaded processing tasks. It is less suited for memory-intensive databases, multi-threaded compilation, or complex parallel workloads due to its weak multi-core and memory bandwidth profiles.
description: The Microsoft Azure D4ls_v5 is a compute-oriented server from the DLSv5 family, featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM on an x86_64 architecture. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz, this virtual machine operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It contains no local storage or GPUs, though it supports premium storage. Performance benchmarks reveal average single-core CPU execution and text processing capabilities, contrasted by weak multi-core scaling and poor memory bandwidth. With a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, this server is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation without high memory demands. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded applications, and basic utility tasks rather than memory-intensive databases or heavy multi-threaded processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4ls_v5 is a DLSv5-family virtual server featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, optimized for low-memory compute workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4ls_v5 is an Intel-based virtual server with 4 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, designed for low-memory compute tasks.
tagline: A dedicated four-core Intel processor instance configured with low memory for cost-effective, single-threaded compute workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory with a low 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
No bundled local storage or graphics processing units
Average single-core performance but weak multi-core and memory bandwidth
Best suited for lightweight web serving and single-threaded utility tasks
